references.title: Advanced Deep Learning Model for Predicting the Academic Performances of Students in Educational Institutions

extracted.Features: [Exam, IN_Sem1, IN_Sem2, IN_Sem3, IN_Sem4, IN_Sem5, IN_Sem6, IN_Sem6, InPc]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors use a model based on GRU. The data is modeled as a having multiple features.

As a start, the authors start with the creation of a fully connected layer which is then given as argument to the GRU, whose outputs are subsequently given to a MaxPooling layer followed by a Dense Layer which does the predictions.
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model: {Accuracy: 0.997 - GRU_Based, Precision: 0.986 - GRU_Based, Recall: 0.963 - GRU_Based, F1-Score: 0.974- GRU_Based}
extracted.Comments:
This paper is not very good. IT presents multiple imprecisions when it comes to techniques (AdaBoost is presented as standard Bagging) and descriptions (k-Fold of 1). 

Authors create a GRU-based model and do benchmarking.
extracted.Limitations:
- Technical errors on description of some of the models (e.g. AdaBoost)

- K-Fold of 1?

- No Ablation study

- Limited exploration of models found in the literature.

annotations.text: Paper focuses on Deep Learning to develop recommender systems of new content. The paper introduces a new model to detect students at risk using GRU. Model was assessed on dataset featuring 16000 records of student assessments from various institutions.

The system was compared against other deep learning based recomender systems. Its unclear to me what is being recommmended though. High accuracy of the model signals the importance of these models in addressing underachievement and potential dropout.
